title: Frameworks Schema
description: Schema for external security framework definitions

id.description: Unique framework identifier used in mapping references

fullName.description: Full official name of the framework

version.description: Framework version. Supports multiple versioning schemes: semantic versioning (e.g., '5.0.1'), year-based (e.g., '2025'), or null for unversioned frameworks

lastUpdated.description: Date framework was last updated (YYYY-MM-DD format)

techniqueUriPattern.description: URI pattern for individual techniques (use {id} as placeholder)

documentUri.description: Direct URI to framework document or specification

applicableTo.description: Entity types that can map to this framework

versionId.description: Generated identifier per ADR-027 D2a/D2b. Concept-id family + '@' + normalized version; bare concept-id when version is null. Materialized by the versionId pre-commit generator (ADR-027 D2b); declared here for the schema-layer charset constraint.

supersedes.description: Prior versionId this version replaces (ADR-027 D2c). Family consistency enforced by the versionId purity validator (ADR-027 D2b).

priorVersions.description: Retired versionIds CoSAI still recognizes as valid pin targets (ADR-027 D2c). Family consistency enforced by the versionId purity validator (ADR-027 D2b).

framework-mapping-patterns.description: Per-framework mapping-ID regex patterns per ADR-022 D5b. Keys mirror the framework.id enum to prevent drift; values constrain the canonical ID shape consumed by risks/controls/personas mappings. ISO 22989 is the deliberate exception (bare string, no regex) because persona ISO 22989 mappings are role descriptors rather than canonical IDs.

framework-mapping-patterns-pinned.description: Per-framework pinned-value mapping patterns per ADR-027 D3a/D6/D7/D8. Pinned form: spec-native canonical ref + framework-determined version token (@ for most, : for OWASP, no token for STRIDE). Non-ID frameworks (iso-22989) use a closed inline enum per D7/D8. This block is the strict target for #343's consumer-schema $ref flip; the legacy framework-mapping-patterns block stays in place during the migration window.

iso-22989.description: Single-member oneOf is the per-edition controlled-vocabulary seam (ADR-027 D7/D8): each future ISO 22989 edition appends a oneOf member carrying that edition's @<version> enum, so existing @2022 pins keep validating. Do not collapse to an inline enum.
